fragments.content: Somatic Symptom Disorder involves one or more distressing somatic symptoms causing significant disruption to daily life, accompanied by excessive thoughts/feelings/behaviors related to symptoms (disproportionate thoughts about seriousness, persistently high anxiety about health/symptoms, excessive time/energy devoted to symptoms). Symptoms may or may not be associated with diagnosed medical condition; the key is the disproportionate response. Related disorders include Illness Anxiety Disorder (preoccupation with having serious illness, minimal/no somatic symptoms), Conversion Disorder (functional neurological symptoms incompatible with recognized conditions), Factitious Disorder (falsification of symptoms for sick role). Prevalence ~5-7%. Pathophysiology involves altered interoceptive awareness, catastrophizing, central sensitization.

Management focuses on improving functioning rather than symptom elimination. Regular scheduled visits with single provider reduce unnecessary testing/procedures. Validate patient experience while gently redirecting from organic focus. CBT addresses catastrophic thinking, reduces healthcare utilization, improves coping. Mindfulness-based therapies help with symptom acceptance. Antidepressants (SSRIs/SNRIs) may help comorbid depression/anxiety and have some evidence for reducing somatic symptom burden. For Conversion Disorder: physical therapy, occupational therapy, psychological treatment addressing stressors. Avoid reinforcing sick role while maintaining therapeutic alliance. Gradual activity increase, focus on function over symptoms. Multidisciplinary approach optimal for complex cases.

DSM-5-TR Somatic Symptom Disorder criteria: One or more somatic symptoms that are distressing or result in significant disruption of daily life. Excessive thoughts/feelings/behaviors related to symptoms as manifested by ≥1: disproportionate/persistent thoughts about symptom seriousness, persistently high level of anxiety about health/symptoms, excessive time/energy devoted to symptoms/health concerns. Although any one symptom may not persist continuously, state of being symptomatic is persistent (typically >6 months). Specifiers: with predominant pain, persistent course, severity (mild/moderate/severe). Illness Anxiety Disorder: preoccupation with having/acquiring serious illness, somatic symptoms not present/mild, high anxiety about health, excessive health-related behaviors or maladaptive avoidance, illness preoccupation ≥6 months.
